answer_raw: 1. DECOMPOSE. Microsoft's FY2025 revenue base is diversified across Server products and cloud services at $98,435 million, Microsoft 365 Commercial products and cloud services at $87,767 million, Gaming at $23,455 million, LinkedIn at $17,812 million, Windows and Devices at $17,314 million, Search and news advertising at $13,878 million, Dynamics products and cloud services at $7,827 million, Enterprise and partner services at $7,760 million, and Microsoft 365 Consumer products and cloud services at $7,404 million. Within that base, Microsoft Cloud revenue was $168.9 billion in FY2025, up 23%, and included Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud, Azure and other cloud services, the commercial portion of LinkedIn, and Dynamics 365. 2. APPLY ECONOMIC FRAMEWORKS. Azure and cloud infrastructure: AI appears to create new demand and complement Azure because Azure and other cloud services revenue grew 34% in FY2025, Azure and other cloud services grew 39% in constant currency in Q2 FY2026, and Satya Nadella said the company added nearly one gigawatt of capacity in the quarter while optimizing for tokens per watt per dollar; however, this unit is supply constrained because management says customer demand continues to exceed supply and datacenters depend on permitted land, predictable energy, networking supplies, servers, GPUs, and other components. Server products: demand is more mixed because FY2025 Server products revenue decreased 3% as customers continued shifting to cloud offerings, while Q2 FY2026 on-premises server revenue increased 21% in constant currency from hybrid solutions and SQL Server 2025, suggesting hybrid demand can remain valuable but is structurally linked to cloud migration. Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud: AI is complementary if Copilot raises ARPU, because Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud revenue grew 15% in FY2025 with seat growth of 6% and revenue-per-user growth, while Q2 FY2026 Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud grew 17% in constant currency and paid Microsoft 365 Commercial seats grew 6% to over 450 million; Satya Nadella also said Microsoft 365 Copilot had 15 million paid seats and seat adds were up over 160% year over year. LinkedIn: AI is positioned as an enhancement rather than a replacement in the disclosures, because the 10-K describes LinkedIn monetized solutions as AI-enabled insights and productivity, FY2025 LinkedIn revenue grew 9%, and Q2 FY2026 LinkedIn revenue increased 11% in constant currency, driven by Marketing Solutions. Dynamics 365: AI is complementary to workflow automation because Dynamics 365 revenue grew 19% in FY2025 and 19% in constant currency in Q2 FY2026, and Satya Nadella cited built-in agents across Dynamics plus customer examples using knowledge-management and sales-qualification agents. Windows and Devices: AI can support demand via AI PCs and Copilot+ PCs, but this unit remains more exposed to PC-market cyclicality; FY2025 Windows OEM and Devices revenue increased only 3%, and Q2 FY2026 Windows OEM and Devices revenue increased 1% while management guided a low-teens decline for the next quarter. Gaming: AI is less central in the cited materials, and the near-term signal is mixed; FY2025 Gaming revenue increased 9% and Xbox content and services revenue increased 16%, but Q2 FY2026 Gaming revenue decreased 9% in constant currency and Xbox content and services revenue decreased 5% in constant currency, below expectations due to first-party content. Search and news advertising: AI can increase engagement but also creates substitution risk because the 10-K describes Bing search with Copilot as key to user acquisition and engagement and FY2025 search and news advertising ex-TAC grew 20%, while the competitive discussion names search engines, websites, social platforms, portals, and in the earnings call Search and news advertising ex-TAC grew 10% in constant currency but was slightly below expectations due to execution challenges. 3. SYNTHESIZE. My long-term thesis is constructive but margin-disciplined: assuming AI value accrues both to infrastructure consumption and to application-layer Copilots, Microsoft's strongest 5+ year revenue engines should be Azure, Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud, Dynamics 365, and security/agent-control-plane offerings, because management reports demand exceeding supply, rapid Copilot adoption, and broad customer use of Foundry, Fabric, Copilot Studio, AgentBuilder, and Agent 365. The offset is capital intensity and gross-margin pressure: FY2025 Microsoft Cloud gross margin percentage decreased to 69% due to scaling AI infrastructure, Q2 FY2026 company gross margin was 68%, down slightly year over year, and Q2 FY2026 capital expenditures were $37.5 billion with roughly two-thirds on short-lived assets such as GPUs and CPUs. Over a 5+ year horizon, I would expect revenue growth to remain AI-supported but return quality to depend on whether Microsoft can keep utilization high, convert capacity into contracted Azure and Copilot revenue, and use efficiency gains to offset depreciation, lease, power, and component-cost pressure; Amy Hood's comments that much GPU capacity is already contracted for most or all of useful life and that margins improve with time on sold capacity support this thesis, but the same comments also make supply planning and customer concentration in major AI contracts material risks.
